commands.review_summary:
The PR adds default-agent generated plugin model catalog inheritance for secondary-agent model discovery, threads it through cached discovery, fingerprints inherited catalogs, and adds ModelRegistry coverage.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +79. Total +151 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main only loads generated plugin catalogs from the active agent directory, and the PR fixture shows an apiKey-only secondary catalog cannot resolve google/gemini-2.5-flash without inheritedAgentDir. I did not execute a local repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainer review should focus on accepting the default-agent catalog inheritance semantics and normal CI results.

target.title: fix(agents): inherit default agent model catalog for secondary agents
target.body:
# fix(agents): inherit default agent model catalog for secondary agents

## What & why

Secondary (non-default) agents created with `openclaw agents add` fail at runtime
with `FailoverError: Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash` when using a
Google/Gemini model authenticated via `GEMINI_API_KEY`. The default/`main` agent
works with the same model and key.

**Root cause:** model discovery only runs for the default agent at gateway
startup, so generated plugin model catalogs (`plugins/<provider>/catalog.json`)
are only populated under the default agent dir. A secondary agent gets an
apiKey-only google catalog with **no models**. `ModelRegistry.loadCustomModels()`
loads plugin catalogs only from the current agent dir, with no read-through to
the default agent — unlike auth profiles, which already inherit via
`inheritedAuthDir`.

## The fix

Add read-through inheritance for generated plugin model catalogs, mirroring the
existing auth-profile read-through:

- `ModelRegistry` accepts an optional `inheritedAgentDir`. When set (and distinct
  from the agent's own dir), its generated plugin catalogs are loaded **first**
  (so any provider request config they register is overridden by the local
  agent's own catalogs — local always wins), and their models are merged as a
  **gap-fill only**: a `(provider, id)` pair already defined locally is never
  overridden.
- A broken/invalid inherited catalog is skipped, never breaking the local agent.
- `discoverModels` threads `inheritedAgentDir` through to the registry.
- `discoverCachedAgentStores` passes the resolved default agent dir, and the
  inherited catalog files are folded into the discovery-cache fingerprint so
  changes to the default agent's catalog invalidate the secondary agent's cached
  snapshot.

The default agent never inherits from itself (guarded by a path-equality check),
so its behavior is unchanged.

## Files

- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts` — `inheritedAgentDir` option + read-through merge
- `src/agents/agent-model-discovery.ts` — thread option through `discoverModels`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model-discovery-cache.ts` — pass default dir + fingerprint
- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts` — 3 new tests

## Real behavior proof

**Before (bug reproduced live), OpenClaw 2026.6.2:**

Secondary agent's catalog had no models:
```
$ cat ~/.openclaw/agents/<secondary>/agent/plugins/google/catalog.json
{ "generatedBy": "openclaw-plugin-model-catalog-v1",
  "providers": { "google": { "apiKey": "GEMINI_API_KEY" } } }
```
Default agent's catalog had the full model list + baseUrl
(`https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta`).

Running a turn on the secondary agent:
```
$ openclaw agent --agent <secondary> -m "hello" --json
GatewayClientRequestError: FailoverError: Unknown model: google/gemini-2.5-flash
```
Same model on `main` returned `"status": "ok"`. Confirmed that
`openclaw models auth --agent <secondary> login --provider google` created the
`google:default` auth profile but the catalog stayed model-less, so the error
persisted.

**After (patched code, run against the real on-disk agent dirs):**

I emptied the secondary agent's google catalog back to the apiKey-only state
that `agents add` leaves behind, then exercised the actual patched discovery path
(`discoverModels`) against the real `~/.openclaw/agents/<secondary>` and `main`
dirs:

```
secondary agent dir : ~/.openclaw/agents/english-tutor/agent   (catalog: apiKey-only, no models)
BEFORE (no inheritance): Unknown model (undefined)  <-- the bug
AFTER  (inherit main)  : google/gemini-2.5-flash  api=google-generative-ai  baseUrl=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta  <-- resolved
```

The inherited model resolves through the **native** `google-generative-ai`
transport (correct provider routing), so the secondary agent can run without any
manual catalog seeding.

**Build + checks (clean):**
```
$ pnpm build        # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo:all     # full prod + test typecheck, all projects — exit 0, 0 errors
$ npx oxlint <4 touched files>   # no warnings/errors
```

**Automated tests (in this PR), run in isolation:**
```
$ npx vitest run src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts \
      src/agents/agent-model-discovery*.test.ts            # 36 passed (10 files)
```
New tests cover: (1) secondary agent inherits the default agent's catalog models,
(2) no inheritance without `inheritedAgentDir` (reproduces the bug), (3) the
secondary agent's own catalog wins over inherited models.

### Not yet tested
- The full repo `pnpm test` via the project runner (`scripts/test-projects.mjs`).
  Note: running the `src/agents/sessions` directory through raw `vitest run`
  surfaces a few unrelated failures (extension loader / package-manager / theme
  tests) that **reproduce with this change reverted** and pass both in isolation
  and in a fully-built checkout — i.e. pre-existing test-isolation flakiness under
  raw `vitest`, not caused by this change.
- A launchd-restarted patched gateway end-to-end over a live channel (the patched
  discovery path itself is proven above against the real on-disk catalogs).
- `pnpm check`'s `npm-shrinkwrap` guard reports a stale shrinkwrap; this is
  pre-existing on `main` and unrelated (this change touches no dependencies).

## AI assistance
Drafted with Claude Code (Opus). Reviewed and validated by me. Session log
available on request.
